QF.id: Introduction to AML, CFT and Proliferation Financing_6

question.text: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) was founded in which year and at whose initiative?

answer.text: 1970, at the initiative of the United States Congress
1988, at the initiative of the Vienna Convention
1989, at the initiative of the G7 Summit in Paris
2001, at the initiative of the United Nations after the 9/11 attacks

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** FATF was founded in 1989 with the initiative of the G7 Summit in Paris. It is an intergovernmental organization whose mission is to devise and promote international standards to prevent money laundering, study ML trends, and monitor compliance.
**The Trap:** 1970 is when the US Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) was enacted — a common confusion. 1988 is the Vienna Convention year. Post-9/11 (2001) is when FATF expanded its mandate to include CFT, but it was already founded 12 years earlier.
**Cross-Exam Context:** FATF's founding year (1989) and its 40 recommendations are standard facts tested in NISM Series XXIV. India became a FATF member in 2010 — another frequently tested fact.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** FATF started with 16 members and grew to 40 by 2023. India is also a member of two FATF-Style Regional Bodies: APG (Asia Pacific Group) and EAG (Eurasian Group).
